thesis_rule: Static pressure rise is not a loss coefficient by itself.
required_fields: p_static_up; p_static_down; dp_hydrostatic; dp_kinetic; pressure average basis; velocity basis
allowed_thesis_wording: A local static pressure increase may reflect hydrostatic correction, kinetic recovery, pressure recovery, or basis choice before irreversible loss is assigned.
forbidden_wording: The corner has a negative loss coefficient because static pressure increases.
target_chapter: Ch. 4 pressure reduction; Ch. 7 hydraulic behavior

thesis_rule: Straight and developing loss must be separated or intentionally retained before naming a coefficient.
required_fields: dp_straight_pipe_correction; reset/development state; tap/plane distance; L/D; f reference convention
allowed_thesis_wording: If straight/developing terms are not isolated, the result is section-effective or diagnostic.
forbidden_wording: The fitting K is valid even though straight loss and recovery are mixed into the taps.
target_chapter: Ch. 4 pressure reduction; Ch. 6 closure admission

thesis_rule: Use component, section, or cluster names according to extraction domain.
allowed_thesis_wording: Use component_K only after isolation and recovery; otherwise use K_section, K_cluster, or diagnostic residual.
forbidden_wording: All corner/two-tap pressure residuals are component K values.
target_chapter: Ch. 4 pressure reduction; Ch. 6 closure admission

thesis_rule: Negative source-defined coefficients require source-matched basis and energy consistency.
required_fields: source definition; pressure basis; velocity basis; flow split; topology; total section balance
allowed_thesis_wording: Negative path coefficients can be reported only as source-defined head-change coefficients with basis and balance stated.
forbidden_wording: Clip all negative K values to zero or call them pressure generation.
target_chapter: Ch. 6 closure admission; Ch. 7 hydraulic behavior

thesis_rule: Reduced-static K-prime and dynamic/total-pressure K are different quantities.
allowed_thesis_wording: Carry both pressure-basis fields before comparing source tables or CFD rows.
forbidden_wording: A reduced-static planar tee coefficient is equivalent to circular-pipe dynamic component K.
target_chapter: Ch. 2 hydraulic closures; Ch. 4 pressure reduction

thesis_rule: Velocity basis controls K magnitude and comparability.
required_fields: u_ref; rho_ref; velocity_basis; area ratio; branch/main basis; U_ref near-zero flag
allowed_thesis_wording: K values are comparable only with explicit upstream/downstream/branch/source-defined velocity basis.
forbidden_wording: A single K table can be compared across all rows without reference velocity definition.
target_chapter: Ch. 4 pressure reduction; Ch. 6 closure admission

thesis_rule: Recirculation invalidates ordinary one-stream component K unless calibrated gates pass.
required_fields: reverse_area_fraction; reverse_mass_fraction; secondary_flow_intensity; stratification; recovery errors; calibration status
allowed_thesis_wording: Recirculating corner rows are model-form evidence until reverse-flow and recovery gates pass.
forbidden_wording: A recirculating two-tap corner row is an ordinary component K row.
target_chapter: Ch. 6 closure admission; Ch. 7 upcomer recirculation

thesis_rule: Residuals are localized before any global multiplier is considered.
required_fields: pressure_residual_assignment; branch_id; fitting_cluster_id; modeled terms; residual norm
allowed_thesis_wording: Use unresolved_minor_loss or cluster_residual when local evidence is incomplete.
forbidden_wording: A global F6/friction multiplier should absorb corner anomalies.
target_chapter: Ch. 6 closure admission; Ch. 8 predictive assessment

thesis_rule: Geometry source envelope is required before source coefficients are imported.
required_fields: part number; geometry class; bend radius; area ratio; junction angle; cluster boundaries
allowed_thesis_wording: Literature coefficients are source-bounded references until TAMU geometry and basis match.
forbidden_wording: Standard bend or tee coefficients can be imported without geometry and basis evidence.
target_chapter: Ch. 2 source envelope; Ch. 6 closure admission

thesis_rule: Same-QOI uncertainty is required for admission.
allowed_thesis_wording: Current pressure-corner rows can be diagnostic without being fit-admitted.
forbidden_wording: A coefficient is admitted without same-QOI pressure/velocity uncertainty.
